# FastAPI Dashboard Coding Standard
# File: .github/skills/fastapi-dashboard.md
# Copilot reads this when building src/api/main.py in Stage 5.

## Required Endpoints
  POST /predict   → run model inference
  GET  /health    → service health check
  GET  /metrics   → all model metrics from JSON file
  GET  /          → full Tailwind HTML dashboard

## /predict Response Schema
  {
    "result":     "ANOMALY" | "NORMAL",
    "confidence": float (0.0 - 1.0),
    "request_id": uuid string,
    "timestamp":  ISO 8601 string
  }

## /health Response Schema
  {
    "status":       "healthy",
    "model_loaded": true,
    "model_name":   "pipeline_model",
    "version":      "1.0"
  }

## Dashboard HTML Requirements
  - Use Tailwind CSS via CDN: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com
  - Dark header bar with project name and green "RUNNING" status badge
  - Live status dot: auto-refreshes every 5 seconds via fetch('/health')
  - Prediction form: one labeled input per feature from feature_schema.json
  - Submit button: POST to /predict, show spinner while waiting
  - Result badge: red "ANOMALY" or green "NORMAL" with confidence %
  - Metrics cards: one card per metric from /metrics endpoint
  - Last 10 predictions table (in-memory list, updated on each predict call)

## Startup Pattern
  from pathlib import Path
  import joblib, json

  ROOT    = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
  MODEL   = joblib.load(ROOT / "models" / "pipeline_model.pkl")
  METRICS = json.loads((ROOT / "models" / "pipeline_model_metrics.json").read_text())
  SCHEMA  = json.loads((ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "feature_schema.json").read_text())

  # SCHEMA is a JSON array of objects: [{"name": "col1", "type": "float", ...}, ...]
  # Extract ordered feature names from schema:
  FEATURE_NAMES = [f["name"] if isinstance(f, dict) else f for f in SCHEMA]

  predictions_log = []  # in-memory last-10 store

## CRITICAL — DO NOT load features.parquet at startup
  The API must NOT import pandas or read any .parquet file at startup.
  features.parquet is a training artefact — it is NOT used for inference.
  Inference uses only: MODEL (the fitted sklearn pipeline) and FEATURE_NAMES (from schema JSON).

## /predict Implementation Pattern
  Use this EXACT pattern — no parquet, no pandas at inference time:

  from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
  from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, HTMLResponse
  import joblib, json, uuid, datetime
  from pathlib import Path

  app = FastAPI(title="ML Pipeline API")

  @app.post("/predict")
  async def predict(request: Request):
      body = await request.json()
      # Build feature vector in the order defined by FEATURE_NAMES
      try:
          values = [float(body.get(name, 0)) for name in FEATURE_NAMES]
      except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:
          return JSONResponse(status_code=422, content={"error": str(e)})
      result_raw = MODEL.predict([values])[0]
      try:
          score = float(MODEL.decision_function([values])[0])
          confidence = round(min(abs(score), 1.0), 4)
      except Exception:
          confidence = round(abs(float(MODEL.score_samples([values])[0])), 4) if hasattr(MODEL, 'score_samples') else 0.5
      label = "ANOMALY" if result_raw == -1 else "NORMAL"
      entry = {
          "result": label,
          "confidence": confidence,
          "request_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
          "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
      }
      predictions_log.append(entry)
      if len(predictions_log) > 10:
          predictions_log.pop(0)
      return entry

## Input validation rule
  If the request body is missing a feature key, default its value to 0.0 — never raise a 422 for missing keys.
  Only raise 422 if a provided value cannot be cast to float.

## Dashboard HTML prediction form generation
  The form inputs are generated by iterating FEATURE_NAMES (the list from SCHEMA).
  Each input is type="number" step="any" with a label matching the feature name.
  Do NOT try to read features.parquet to get column names.

## Dependency note
  requirements.txt must include: fastapi uvicorn joblib scikit-learn
  Do NOT include pandas or pyarrow as required for the API to run.
